data.content:
### Install Payload Lexical Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/overview.mdx

This command installs the necessary `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical` package using pnpm, which is required to integrate the Lexical rich text editor into your Payload CMS project.

```bash
pnpm install @payloadcms/richtext-lexical
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Lexical Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-lexical/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical` package using npm, which provides the Lexical Rich Text Editor plugin for Payload CMS. This is a prerequisite for using the editor in your Payload project.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/richtext-lexical
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Slate Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-slate/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-slate` package, which provides the Slate-based rich text editor functionality for Payload CMS projects. Run this command in your project's terminal to add the dependency.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/richtext-slate
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Slate Rich Text Editor via npm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/slate.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-slate` package, which provides the deprecated Slate-based rich text editor for Payload CMS. It should be saved as a production dependency.

```bash
npm install --save @payloadcms/richtext-slate
```

--------------------------------

### Install core Payload packages in an existing app with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

Installs the essential Payload CMS packages, including the core Payload library, Next.js integration, Lexical rich text editor, image processing with sharp, and GraphQL. This command uses pnpm; users should substitute with their preferred package manager (e.g., npm, yarn) and consider `--legacy-peer-deps` for npm if needed.

```bash
pnpm i payload @payloadcms/next @payloadcms/richtext-lexical sharp graphql
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Nested Docs Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/nested-docs.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/plugin-nested-docs` package using pnpm, a JavaScript package manager, making the plugin available for use in your Payload CMS project. Alternative package managers like npm or Yarn can also be used.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-nested-docs
```

--------------------------------

### Install PayloadCMS Search Plugin via Package Manager

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/search.mdx

Install the @payloadcms/plugin-search package using pnpm, npm, or yarn package managers. This plugin enables fast document search by creating an indexed search collection in your database.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-search
```

--------------------------------

### Install SEO Plugin (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/seo.mdx

Install the SEO plugin using a JavaScript package manager such as pnpm, npm, or Yarn. This command adds the necessary package to your project dependencies.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-seo
```

--------------------------------

### Install PayloadCMS Translations Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/configuration/i18n.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/translations` package, which is required to enable internationalization (I18n) support in your Payload CMS project. It provides various language files and utilities necessary for translating the admin interface.

```bash
pnpm install @payloadcms/translations
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload SDK Package with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rest-api/overview.mdx

This command demonstrates how to add the `@payloadcms/sdk` package to your project using the pnpm package manager. This package provides a type-safe way to interact with the Payload REST API.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Optional Sharp Dependency for Image Processing (PNPM & TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/migration-guide/overview.mdx

Installs the `sharp` package, which is now an optional dependency for Payload 3.0's image processing features like `formatOptions`, `imageSizes`, or `resizeOptions`. After installation, `sharp` must be explicitly added to the `payload.config.ts` to enable its functionality.

```bash
pnpm i sharp
```

```typescript
// payload.config.ts
import sharp from 'sharp'
buildConfig({
// ...
+   sharp,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Convert Lexical Richtext to Plaintext in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converting-plaintext.mdx

Demonstrates basic conversion of richtext data to plaintext using the convertLexicalToPlaintext function from @payloadcms/richtext-lexical/plaintext package. Takes a SerializedEditorState object as input and returns a plaintext string representation of the richtext content.

```typescript
import type { SerializedEditorState } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/lexical'

import { convertLexicalToPlaintext } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/plaintext'

// Your richtext data here
const data: SerializedEditorState = {}

const plaintext = convertLexicalToPlaintext({ data })
```

--------------------------------

### Outline Simple Payload Plugin Package Structure

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/PLUGIN-DEVELOPMENT.md

This snippet illustrates a basic and common package structure for a Payload CMS plugin. It includes essential files like `package.json` for metadata, `README.md` for documentation, `LICENSE.md`, and a `src` directory containing the main entry point (`index.ts`), plugin implementation (`plugin.ts`), and type definitions (`types.ts`). Optional `exports` can provide additional entry points for specific types.

```plaintext
plugin-<name>/
├── package.json              # Package metadata and dependencies
├── README.md                 # Plugin documentation
├── LICENSE.md                # License file
└── src/
    ├── index.ts              # Entry point, re-exports plugin and config types
    ├── plugin.ts             # Plugin implementation
    ├── types.ts              # TypeScript type definitions
    └── exports/              # Additional entry points (optional)
        └── types.ts          # Type-only exports
```

--------------------------------

### Install MCP Plugin using package manager

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/mcp.mdx

Command to install the @payloadcms/plugin-mcp package using pnpm. This plugin can also be installed with npm or Yarn package managers. The plugin is currently in Beta and available on npm.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-mcp
```

--------------------------------

### Install Import Export Plugin via Package Manager

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/import-export.mdx

Installs the @payloadcms/plugin-import-export package using pnpm. This plugin is in beta and currently supports only exporting of collection data. The same installation can be done with npm or yarn package managers.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-import-export
```

--------------------------------

### Payload Plugin package.json Configuration

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/PLUGIN-DEVELOPMENT.md

This JSON configuration file defines a Payload CMS plugin's metadata, build scripts, dependencies, and export points. It specifies build commands using SWC and TypeScript, testing configurations with Vitest and Playwright, and peer dependencies on the core Payload package.

```json
{
  "name": "payload-plugin-example",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A Payload CMS plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./dist/index.js",
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/index.js"
    },
    "./types": {
      "import": "./dist/exports/types.js",
      "types": "./dist/exports/types.d.ts"
    },
    "./client": {
      "import": "./dist/exports/client.js",
      "types": "./dist/exports/client.d.ts"
    },
    "./rsc": {
      "import": "./dist/exports/rsc.js",
      "types": "./dist/exports/rsc.d.ts"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "npm run copyfiles && npm run build:types && npm run build:swc",
    "build:swc": "swc ./src -d ./dist --config-file .swcrc --strip-leading-paths",
    "build:types": "tsc --emitDeclarationOnly --outDir dist",
    "clean": "rimraf dist *.tsbuildinfo",
    "copyfiles": "copyfiles -u 1 \"src/**/*.{html,css,scss,ttf,woff,woff2,eot,svg,jpg,png,json}\" dist/",
    "dev": "next dev dev --turbo",
    "dev:generate-types": "cross-env PAYLOAD_CONFIG_PATH=./dev/payload.config.ts payload generate:types",
    "dev:payload": "cross-env PAYLOAD_CONFIG_PATH=./dev/payload.config.ts payload",
    "test": "npm run test:int && npm run test:e2e",
    "test:int": "vitest",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "lint:fix": "eslint ./src --fix",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run clean && npm run build"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@payloadcms/translations": "^3.0.0",
    "@payloadcms/ui": "^3.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@payloadcms/db-mongodb": "^3.0.0",
    "@payloadcms/next": "^3.0.0",
    "@payloadcms/richtext-lexical": "^3.0.0",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.40.0",
    "@swc/cli": "^0.1.62",
    "@swc/core": "^1.3.0",
    "copyfiles": "^2.4.1",
    "cross-env": "^7.0.3",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "next": "^15.0.0",
    "payload": "^3.0.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "vitest": "^3.0.0"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "payload": "^3.0.0"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Lexical Rich Text Editor in Payload's Root Config

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/overview.mdx

This TypeScript example shows how to globally enable the Lexical editor for your Payload CMS project. You import `buildConfig` and `lexicalEditor`, then pass `lexicalEditor({})` to the `editor` property within your top-level Payload configuration.

```ts
import { buildConfig } from 'payload'
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export default buildConfig({
  collections: [
    // your collections here
  ],
  // Pass the Lexical editor to the root config
  editor: lexicalEditor({}),
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Admin Bar package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/admin-bar/README.md

Installs the `@payloadcms/admin-bar` package using pnpm. This package is a dependency for using the Payload Admin Bar React component in your project.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/admin-bar
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload to Use Lexical Rich Text Editor (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-lexical/README.md

This TypeScript code demonstrates how to integrate the Lexical Rich Text Editor into your Payload CMS configuration. It imports the necessary `buildConfig` and `lexicalEditor` functions, then sets the `editor` property within your Payload configuration to `lexicalEditor({})` to enable the plugin.

```ts
import { buildConfig } from 'payload'
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export default buildConfig({
  editor: lexicalEditor({}),
  // ...rest of config
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel Blob Storage for PayloadCMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/storage-vercel-blob/README.md

Install the necessary package using pnpm to add Vercel Blob storage capabilities to your PayloadCMS project. This command fetches and installs the '@payloadcms/storage-vercel-blob' package.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/storage-vercel-blob
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe SDK package for Payload CMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/ecommerce/plugin.mdx

Before using the Stripe adapter in Payload, the Stripe SDK package needs to be installed manually, as Payload does not automatically include it. This command uses pnpm to add the 'stripe' package to your project.

```shell
pnpm add stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Outline Exhaustive Payload Plugin Package Structure with Advanced Features

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/PLUGIN-DEVELOPMENT.md

This comprehensive package structure, derived from the official Payload plugin template, details an advanced setup for Payload CMS plugins. It includes compiler configurations (`.swcrc`, `tsconfig.json`), linting (`eslint.config.js`), testing (`vitest.config.js`, `playwright.config.js`), and dedicated directories within `src` for advanced features like custom API endpoints (`endpoints/`), React components (`components/`), custom fields (`fields/`), default values (`defaults.ts`), internationalization (`translations/`), and specific export types (`exports/`). This structure supports robust and feature-rich plugin development.

```plaintext
plugin-<name>/
├── .swcrc                    # SWC compiler config
├── package.json              # Package metadata and dependencies
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript config
├── README.md                 # Plugin documentation
├── LICENSE.md                # License file
├── eslint.config.js          # ESLint configuration (optional)
├── vitest.config.js          # Vitest test configuration (optional)
├── playwright.config.js      # Playwright e2e tests (optional)
└── src/
    ├── index.ts              # Entry point, re-exports plugin and config types
    ├── plugin.ts             # Plugin implementation
    ├── types.ts              # TypeScript type definitions
    ├── defaults.ts           # Default configuration values (optional)
    ├── endpoints/            # Custom API endpoints (optional)
    │   └── handler.ts
    ├── components/           # React components (optional)
    │   ├── ClientComponent.tsx    # 'use client' components
    │   └── ServerComponent.tsx    # RSC components
    ├── fields/               # Custom field components (optional)
    │   ├── FieldName/
    │   │   ├── index.ts      # Field config
    │   │   └── Component.tsx # Client component
    ├── exports/              # Additional entry points
    │   ├── types.ts          # Type-only exports
    │   ├── fields.ts         # Field-only exports
    │   ├── client.ts         # Re-export client components
    │   └── rsc.ts            # Re-export server components (RSC)
    ├── translations/         # i18n translations (optional)
    │   └── index.ts
    └── ui/                   # Admin UI components (optional)
        └── Component.tsx
```

--------------------------------

### Importing Lexical Editor Config Utilities (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to import `SanitizedConfig` from Payload and `editorConfigFactory` from `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical`. These imports are essential for working with Lexical editor configurations within a Payload project.

```ts
import type { SanitizedConfig } from 'payload'
import { editorConfigFactory } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

// Your Payload Config needs to be available in order to retrieve the default editor config
const config: SanitizedConfig = {} as SanitizedConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Install SQLite Database Adapter for Payload with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

This command installs the official SQLite adapter for Payload CMS, enabling your application to connect and interact with an SQLite database. It uses pnpm for package management; adjust for your specific package manager if necessary.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/db-sqlite
```

--------------------------------

### Install MongoDB Database Adapter for Payload with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

This command installs the official MongoDB adapter for Payload CMS, enabling your application to connect and interact with a MongoDB database. It uses pnpm for package management; replace pnpm with your package manager if different.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/db-mongodb
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Lexical Editor Config in Payload `afterRead` Hook (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This comprehensive example demonstrates how to find a `RichTextField` within `siblingFields` in a collection's `afterRead` hook and then extract its Lexical editor configuration using `editorConfigFactory.fromField`. This pattern allows dynamic access to editor settings based on field data, providing flexibility for content processing.

```ts
import type { CollectionConfig, RichTextField } from 'payload'

import {
  editorConfigFactory,
  getEnabledNodes,
  lexicalEditor,
} from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export const MyCollection: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'slug',
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'text',
      type: 'text',
      hooks: {
        afterRead: [
          ({ siblingFields, value }) => {
            const field: RichTextField = siblingFields.find(
              (field) => 'name' in field && field.name === 'richText',
            ) as RichTextField

            const editorConfig = editorConfigFactory.fromField({
              field,
            })

            // Now you can use the editor config

            return value
          },
        ],
      },
    },
    {
      name: 'richText',
      type: 'richText',
      editor: lexicalEditor(),
    },
  ],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload Collection for Slate-to-Lexical Preview

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

This TypeScript example demonstrates how to integrate the `SlateToLexicalFeature` into a `richText` field within a Payload CMS `CollectionConfig`. By adding this feature to the Lexical editor's features array, data from Slate is converted on-the-fly during read operations, allowing for a preview of the migration without modifying the database. This setup is crucial for testing content conversion before permanent migration.

```ts
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload';

import { SlateToLexicalFeature } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/migrate';
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical';

const Pages: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'pages',
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'content',
      type: 'richText',
      editor: lexicalEditor({
        features: ({ defaultFeatures }) => [
          ...defaultFeatures,
          SlateToLexicalFeature({}),
        ],
      }),
    },
  ],
};
```

--------------------------------

### Install PayloadCMS Live Preview Package (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/live-preview/server.mdx

Installs the base `@payloadcms/live-preview` package using npm. This package provides essential functions like `ready` and `isDocumentEvent` required for integrating live preview capabilities into any front-end framework.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/live-preview
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Lexical Editor Config from a Field (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This snippet illustrates how to extract the editor configuration directly from an existing Lexical rich text field object. It utilizes `editorConfigFactory.fromField` and requires a field configuration object as input.

```ts
const fieldEditorConfig = editorConfigFactory.fromField({
  field: config.collections[0].fields[1],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Postgres Database Adapter for Payload with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

This command installs the official PostgreSQL adapter for Payload CMS, allowing your application to connect and interact with a PostgreSQL database. It uses pnpm for package management; ensure to use your package manager if it's not pnpm.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/db-postgres
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Lexical Editor features with custom link fields, upload fields, and Payload blocks

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/overview.mdx

This TypeScript example demonstrates how to initialize a PayloadCMS Lexical Editor, specifying various features. It shows customization of the `LinkFeature` to add a custom 'rel' attribute field, customization of the `UploadFeature` to include a rich text caption field for uploads, and integration of custom Payload `BlocksFeature` like Banner and CallToAction. This setup allows for extending the editor's capabilities with custom UI and content structures.

```typescript
import {
  BlocksFeature,
  LinkFeature,
  UploadFeature,
  lexicalEditor,
} from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
import { Banner } from '../blocks/Banner'
import { CallToAction } from '../blocks/CallToAction'

{
  editor: lexicalEditor({
    features: ({ defaultFeatures, rootFeatures }) => [
      ...defaultFeatures,
      LinkFeature({
        // Example showing how to customize the built-in fields
        // of the Link feature
        fields: ({ defaultFields }) => [
          ...defaultFields,
          {
            name: 'rel',
            label: 'Rel Attribute',
            type: 'select',
            hasMany: true,
            options: ['noopener', 'noreferrer', 'nofollow'],
            admin: {
              description:
                'The rel attribute defines the relationship between a linked resource and the current document. This is a custom link field.',
            },
          },
        ],
      }),
      UploadFeature({
        collections: {
          uploads: {
            // Example showing how to customize the built-in fields
            // of the Upload feature
            fields: [
              {
                name: 'caption',
                type: 'richText',
                editor: lexicalEditor(),
              },
            ],
          },
        },
      }),
      // This is incredibly powerful. You can reuse your Payload blocks
      // directly in the Lexical editor as follows:
      BlocksFeature({
        blocks: [Banner, CallToAction],
      }),
    ],
  })
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe Package for Payload CMS Ecommerce Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/ecommerce/payments.mdx

This command installs the `stripe` npm package, which is a required dependency for integrating Stripe payment processing with the Payload CMS Ecommerce Plugin. Ensure you install version `18.5.0` or higher for optimal compatibility.

```bash
  pnpm add stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Configure a Basic Rich Text Field with Lexical Editor in Payload CMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/FIELDS.md

This example illustrates how to define a `richText` field using the Lexical editor in Payload CMS. It shows basic configuration, including enabling specific features like headings and links by extending the default feature set.

```ts
import type { RichTextField } from 'payload'
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
import { HeadingFeature, LinkFeature } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

const richTextField: RichTextField = {
  name: 'content',
  type: 'richText',
  required: true,
  localized: true,
  editor: lexicalEditor({
    features: ({ defaultFeatures }) => [
      ...defaultFeatures,
      HeadingFeature({
        enabledHeadingSizes: ['h1', 'h2', 'h3'],
      }),
      LinkFeature({
        enabledCollections: ['posts', 'pages'],
      }),
    ],
  }),
}
```

--------------------------------

### Override Lexical Editor Settings for a Specific Field in Payload

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/overview.mdx

This TypeScript code demonstrates how to apply the Lexical editor to a particular `richText` field within a collection, allowing for field-specific customization. By passing `lexicalEditor({})` directly to the field's `editor` property, you can override global settings.

```ts
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export const Pages: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'pages',
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'content',
      type: 'richText',
      // Pass the Lexical editor here and override base settings as necessary
      editor: lexicalEditor({}),
    },
  ],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Prepare and Preview Payload Docs Locally via Shell

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

This section provides shell commands to set up the Payload website repository, install dependencies, configure the `DOCS_DIR` environment variable, fetch local documentation, and start the development server for previewing changes. It assumes a pnpm environment.

```shell
pnpm install
```

```shell
DOCS_DIR=/Users/yourname/Documents/GitHub/payload/docs
```

```shell
pnpm fetchDocs:local
```

```shell
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload CMS Lexical Rich Text Editor to Customize Placeholder

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/overview.mdx

This configuration snippet shows how to customize the placeholder text displayed in an empty Payload CMS Lexical rich text editor. By setting the `placeholder` prop within the `admin` object of the `lexicalEditor` configuration, users will see the specified text when the editor is empty, improving user guidance.

```TypeScript
{
  name: 'richText',
  type: 'richText',
  editor: lexicalEditor({
    admin: {
      placeholder: 'Type your content here...'
    },
  }),
}
```
